codeReview.summary: Feature remains non-compliant with VAL-HEADERS-004: runtime HTTPS write timeout is still multi-minute on default configuration; this commit only adds tests and one test codifies non-30s behavior.

issues.description: HTTPS write timeout is computed as max(30s, ColdStartTimeout+15s). With default ColdStartTimeout=2m (internal/config/snapshot.go:17) passed into edgeproxy.New (cmd/kindling/serve_edge.go:134), default runtime timeout becomes 2m15s instead of the required 30s (VAL-HEADERS-004).

CORS middleware returns OPTIONS requests before calling next, while SecurityHeadersMiddleware is inside CORS (lines 209 and 214). As a result, preflight API responses do not receive security headers despite the feature's "all API responses" wording.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The security-backend-worker skill does not explicitly require validating config-driven defaults through real startup/default paths. This can allow assertion-level test coverage to pass while contract-required default runtime behavior remains wrong.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SKILL.md step 3 is generic; transcript marks feature complete, but added tests in internal/edgeproxy/edgeproxy_security_test.go (lines 456-488) use hand-picked ColdStartTimeout values and even assert expansion, instead of validating the default startup path wired by cmd/kindling/serve_edge.go:114-134 and internal/config/snapshot.go:17.

summary: Reviewed handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit diff for security-response-headers. The feature fails review because default HTTPS write timeout remains 2m15s on default config, violating VAL-HEADERS-004's 30s requirement. Added tests improved coverage but did not correct the runtime behavior.
